conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your situation, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have any children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you please tell me their ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do have a child. My son Alex is 17 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I need to understand a bit more about Alex's current situation.

**Is Alex currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training?**

(For example, is he studying for A-levels, Scottish Highers, or similar qualifications at school or college, or is he in approved vocational training?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment. He's finished school and isn't studying anymore.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I have a few more questions to assess your eligibility.

**Is Alex currently registered with a careers service or with Connexions (or similar service), and is he waiting to start approved training or education?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he hasn't registered with any careers service or anything like that. He just left school and hasn't signed up with anyone.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Now I need to ask about your residency and responsibility for Alex.

**Do you live in the UK, and does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - we're in Manchester. And yes, Alex lives here at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I have one more important question:

**Are you responsible for Alex? This means are you the one who looks after him and pays for things like his food, clothes, and other costs?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for him - I look after him and pay for everything he needs like food, clothes, and all his other costs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I'm afraid you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

Here's why:

Child Benefit can be claimed for:
- Children under 16 years old, OR
- Young people aged 16-19 who are in approved full-time non-advanced education or approved training

Since Alex is 17 and has left school without continuing in any education or training, and hasn't registered with a careers service while waiting to start approved training, he doesn't meet the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.

**However, please note:** If Alex's situation changes and he decides to return to education (like studying for A-levels or similar qualifications) or starts approved training, you could become eligible for Child Benefit again. In that case, you would need to inform Child Benefit of the change in circumstances.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit or Alex's situation?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 17 years old and is not in full-time non-advanced education or approved training. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you can claim Child Benefit for children under 16, or under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex has left school and is not in any education, training, or registered with careers services waiting to start approved training, he does not meet the age and education requirements for Child Benefit. Child Benefit eligibility ends when a young person turns 16 unless they continue in qualifying education or training, which Alex is not doing.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 17-year-old son Alex, for whom they are responsible and provide financial support. However, Alex has left school and is not currently in any education, training, or registered with careers services. While children under 16 automatically qualify for Child Benefit, those aged 16-19 must be in approved full-time non-advanced education or approved training to remain eligible. Since Alex does not meet this requirement, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
